observations.observation: Token velocity held at 0.604x with acceleration at 1.002x — essentially flat for the third consecutive heartbeat. After the post-blackout recovery to 0.603x, the system is in a sustained low-acceleration plateau: growing, but barely. The burst-quiet dynamic appears to apply to velocity as well as geography.

8 new CBSAs appeared this snapshot (19820, 37980, 15380, 22220, 45300, non-metro KY, non-metro ME, 38860), pushing total active metros to 24 and geo diffusion rate to 33.3%. This is the second time an 8-CBSA burst has occurred — the burst pattern is recurring, not a one-time event.

Token velocity held at 0.603x with acceleration at 1.001x — essentially flat, the second consecutive heartbeat at this level after recovering from the post-blackout dip. Only 1 new receipt and $15.20 new GMV this cycle, with 0 new CBSAs and 0 settlement payouts — the network is in a quiet consolidation phase after the 16-CBSA burst two heartbeats ago.

GMV reached $226,708 across 3,883 receipts this snapshot. 7-day trailing window is $29,333 across 496 receipts — the 7d window is growing, suggesting sustained intake pace rather than a one-time burst.

Non-metro Missouri is the latest CBSA to appear, continuing the rural diffusion pattern. Active CBSAs now at 16, with geo diffusion rate at 6.3% (1 new this snapshot). The network's rural expansion — non-metro KY, IL, TX, MI, WI, NY, MO — now spans 7 distinct non-metro state designations, suggesting geographic spread is following state-level social graphs rather than metro density.

Token velocity recovered to 0.603x this snapshot (up from 0.563x last heartbeat), with acceleration at 1.071x — the system is back above the prior 0.597x floor after the post-blackout dip. 5 new receipts and $44.84 new GMV in 3 hours, with 1 new CBSA (non-metro Missouri).

The vendor frontier shows 5 pending GitHub requests — all developer infrastructure (Notion, Render, Vercel, DigitalOcean, Hetzner) — while the actual DKIM vendor list activated this snapshot is dominated by AI/SaaS (anthropic.com, openai.com, gamma.app, suno.com) and the token dataset is dominated by physical retail (Walmart, Kroger, Dollar General, Home Depot). Three distinct adoption cohorts are signaling simultaneously with no overlap.

GMV crossed both $100K and $200K milestones in the delta window, now sitting at $226,663 across 3,878 receipts. Receipt count also crossed 1K, 2K, and 3K thresholds — multiple milestone crossings in a single snapshot suggest the data was accumulating during the 2-heartbeat blackout and is now surfacing.

Token velocity dropped to 0.563x this snapshot (down from 0.630x last heartbeat), with acceleration returning null — the system came back online after a 2-heartbeat blackout but is decelerating. The recovery from the 0.597x floor appears to have stalled; velocity is now below that prior floor.

16 new CBSAs appeared in a single snapshot — 100% of active metros are new this window, pushing geo diffusion rate to 1.0 (maximum). This is the largest single-heartbeat geographic expansion recorded, surpassing the prior record of 8 metros. The burst includes non-metro Wisconsin, Illinois, New York, and 12 named CBSAs — the network is no longer metro-centric.
